instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-all-plusp` that takes any number of expressions, evaluates each once, and signals an error naming the source form for any one that is not strictly positive.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro assert-all-plusp (&rest exprs)
  `(progn
     ,@(mapcar (lambda (e)
                 (let ((g (gensym)))
                   `(let ((,g ,e))
                      (unless (plusp ,g) (error "Expected positive, got ~A from ~S" ,g ',e)))))
               exprs)))
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (PROGN
           (LET ((#:G1 X))
             (UNLESS (PLUSP #:G1)
               (ERROR "Expected positive, got ~A from ~S" #:G1 'X)))
           (LET ((#:G2 Y))
             (UNLESS (PLUSP #:G2)
               (ERROR "Expected positive, got ~A from ~S" #:G2 'Y))))
ACTUAL:   (PROGN
           (LET ((#:G112 X))
             (UNLESS (PLUSP #:G112)
               (ERROR "Expected positive, got ~A from ~S" #:G112 'X)))
           (LET ((#:G113 Y))
             (UNLESS (PLUSP #:G113)
               (ERROR "Expected positive, got ~A from ~S" #:G113 'Y))))
